mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 50% accuracy over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns are uncorrelated with the product of RSAFS and HOUST growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term profit warnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where INDPRO falls below its 12-month average, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate restrictive real rates that suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS levels shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year sample, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction that forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and payroll growth, where output expands faster than employment, signals productivity gains that boost profit margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The productivity divergence signal yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day returns are uncorrelated with the INDPRO-to-PAYEMS growth differential.
